Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Cultural Properties in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Cultural Properties in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Cultural Properties in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Cultural Properties in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-typed Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-typed Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-typed Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-typed design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-typed mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-typed kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Cultural Properties in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designated as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designations]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the former Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designations]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designations]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |cultural_property=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] -
