2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designations]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |group={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designations]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:dd]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New3]]

Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == Cultural Property == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town Cultural Property Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is other_designation1 as a Fukushima Prefecture other_designation. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with addition[[Category:addition]]al Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an addition[[Category:addition]]al 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of other_designation in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * other_designation Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from other_designations" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}}

Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|12=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kinegamori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|12=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|12=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|12=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Archaeology}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|12=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kinegamori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Archaeology}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 杵ガ森古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所属 = [[宇内青津古墳群]] |所在地 = [[福島県]][[河沼郡]][[会津坂下町]]稲荷塚 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=杵ガ森古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長45.6m
高さ2m |築造年代 = [[3世紀]]末-[[4世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = [[土師器]] |指定文化財 = 福島県指定史跡「杵ガ森古墳」 |特記事項 = 東北地方最古の古墳か }} '''杵ガ森古墳'''（きねがもりこふん）は、[[福島県]][[河沼郡]][[会津坂下町]]にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[宇内青津古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。福島県指定史跡に指定されている。 東北地方で最古に位置づけられうる古墳として知られる。 == 概要 == 会津坂下町の市街地西側、旧宮川（鶴沼川）西岸に位置する前方後円墳で{{Sfn|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}、前方部を南方に向ける。周辺には臼ガ森古墳や米ガ森古墳（現在は消滅）も築かれ{{Sfn|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}、一帯が古墳時代前期に繁栄した様子が見られる。 後円部は2段築成であるが、前方部は削られており詳細は不明。前方部がバチ型を成す墳形は、日本最古の前方後円墳である[[箸墓古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]）と類似するものであり、規模は箸墓古墳の約6分の1になる。また、古墳の周囲にほぼ相似形の周堀が巡らされている。 本古墳は『新編会津風土記』に塚としての記載が見えるが史跡説明板。、[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）の遺跡分布調査の際に発見され公式に認識された{{Sfn|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}。後に行われる発掘調査までは、後円部の一部のみを地上に出し、その他は水田の下に埋まっている状態であった。[[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）の区画整理事業に伴って実施された発掘調査において、それまで[[円墳]]と考えられていた本古墳が前方後円墳であることがわかり、古墳の規模も併せて判明した{{Sfn|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}。その調査では、古墳周囲の周堀のほか、墳丘直下や周囲からは[[竪穴建物]]跡や[[方形周溝墓]]群10基等も検出された{{Sfn|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}。埋葬施設は明らかとなっていないが、出土した土師器から3世紀末-4世紀初頭頃の築造と推定され、東北地方では最古級の古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1996年]]（平成8年）に福島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：45.6メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：25.6メートル ** 高さ：約2メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：24メートル ** 幅：19メートル == 稲荷塚遺跡 == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|稲荷塚遺跡]] '''稲荷塚遺跡'''（いなりづかいせき）は、杵ガ森古墳付近に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の遺跡。 [[1990年|1990]]-[[1991年]]（平成2-3年）に行われた調査で、[[竪穴建物]]跡15軒、[[掘立柱建物]]跡3棟、[[方形周溝墓]]10基等が発見された。これらの位置づけとして、建物群が古墳時代前期初頭に集落として機能したのち、その跡地上に杵ガ森古墳が築かれ、さらにその周囲に杵ガ森古墳ゆかりの人物の方形周溝墓が営まれたと見られている。 [[2017年]]（平成29年）にはさらに方形周溝墓13基が発見されており、周溝墓は計23基を数え福島県内では最大級の周溝墓群になるとされる[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20171122-222147.php "稲荷塚遺跡から福島県最大級「方形周溝墓群」 新たに13基発見"]（福島民友、2017年11月22日記事）。。 == 文化財 == === 福島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 杵ガ森古墳 - 1996年（平成8年）3月22日指定[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html 町内の文化財一覧]（会津坂下町ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=杵ガ森古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 7 福島県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|杵ガ森古墳（平凡社）|1993年}}}} * 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029、新泉社、2006年 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=坂下西第一地区発掘調査概報 -杵ガ森古墳・稲荷塚遺跡-（会津坂下町文化財調査報告書 第22集）|publisher=会津坂下町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=会津坂下町杵ガ森古墳・稲荷塚遺跡発掘調査報告書（会津坂下町文化財調査報告書 第33集）|publisher=会津坂下町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=会津坂下町杵ガ森古墳保存整備報告書|publisher=会津坂下町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html 杵ガ森古墳] - 会津坂下町ホームページ {{考古学}} {{デフォルトソート:きねかもりこふん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:会津坂下町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:福島県指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|杵ガ森古墳|version=92335937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd deprecated open]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]]
