This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=}}, {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizubange|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods={{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizubange|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}, in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=}}) is an early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 {{ill|pillar-supported buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pillar-supported buildings|qq=}}, and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|杵ガ森古墳|version=92335937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:会津坂下町の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kinegamori-kofun}}

Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211679]] Wikidata name: Kinegamori-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37|33|58.73|N|139|48|34.89|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kinegamori Kofun}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|杵ガ森古墳|version=92335937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kinegamori-kofun}}

Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211679]] Wikidata name: Kinegamori-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kinegamori Kofun |image=[[File:Kinegamori-kofun zenkei.JPG|280px]]
Complete view of the Kofun (front section in foreground, rear section in back right) |part_of={{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Inarizuka, Aizubange|ja|稲荷塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka, Aizubange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] |coordinates={{Coord|37.566314|N|139.809692|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size=45.6 m mound length
2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - Aizubange Town website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|杵ガ森古墳|version=92335937|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kinegamori Kofun]] [[ja:杵ガ森古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kinegamori-kofun}}

Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] - 2 m height |constructed=Late 3rd to early 4th century |burial=Unknown |buried= |burial goods=[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |notes=Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Kinegamori Kofun" |notes=Possibly the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kinegamori Kofun''' ({{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}} and is Historic Site1 as a Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. Kinegamori Kofun is recognized as one of the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region. == Overview == Located west of the city center of Aizubange, along the western bank of the typeer Miyagawa River (Tsurunumagawa), this keyhole-shaped Kofun faces south. Surrounding it are other ancient Kofuns, including Usagamori Kofun and Maigamori Kofun (now disappeared), which illustrate the region's prosperity during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The rear section of Kinegamori Kofun is a two-tiered construction, while the details of the front section are unclear due to erosion. Its distinctive paddle-shaped design resembles that of the earliest keyhole-shaped mound, [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]], in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], though Kinegamori is about one-sixth the size of Hashihaka. The mound is encircled by a moat. The mound was first noted in the historical text ''Shinpen Aizu Fudoki'' as a mound, but it was officially identified during an archaeological survey in 1961. Before excavations, only part of the rear section was visible, with the remainder buried under rice fields. Excavations during 1990 revealed that the mound, previously thought to be circular, was actually a keyhole-shaped kofun. Additional findings included a surrounding moat, pit dwellings, and 10 {{ill|Square Kofuns|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] suggest the mound was constructed in the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the oldest kofun in Tohoku. == area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 45.6 meters * Rear section: ** Diameter: 25.6 meters ** Height: ~2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 24 meters ** Width: 19 meters == Inarizuka Site == [[File:Inarizuka-iseki (Aizubange).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Inarizuka Site]] The '''Inarizuka Site''' ({{ill|Inarizuka Site|ja|稲荷塚遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] site near Kinegamori Kofun. Excavations from 1990 to 1991 uncovered 15 pit dwellings, 3 [[:en:Post in ground|pillar-supported buildings]], and 10 square Kofuns. These findings suggest that the site initially functioned as a settlement during the early Kofun period. Kinegamori Kofun was later constructed on the site, with additional Kofuns built nearby for individuals associated with the kofun. In 2017, an additional 13 square Kofuns were discovered, bringing the total to 23, making it the largest collection of square Kofuns in Fukushima Prefecture[http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM2017dd22-222147.php "Fukushima Prefecture’s Largest 'Square Kofun Group' Discovered at Inarizuka Site"] (''Minyu'', November 22, 2017).. == designation1 == === Fukushima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kinegamori Kofun - Designated on March 22, 1996[http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/285.html "List of Historic Site in Aizubange"] (Aizubange Town website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in this article}} * Historic Site Description Panel * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Kinegamori Kofun|title={{ill|Historical Place names of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Place names of Japan|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 7: Fukushima Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490077|ref={{Harvid|Kinegamori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1993}}}} * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historic Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not directly used in the article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=Excavation Report on Kinegamori Kofun and Inarizuka Site (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 22)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=|title=Preservation Report of Kinegamori Kofun (Aizubange Town designation1 Report No. 33)|publisher=Aizubange Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kinegamori-kofun}} * [http://www.town.aizubange.fukushima.jp/soshiki/30/282.html Kinegamori Kofun] -
